Premium Japanese Matcha Sourcing for North American Cafés and Brands

Origin intelligence, sourcing research, and supplier guidance for operators who need to get it right.

Read the Buyer Journal
Demand
¥72.1B
Japan green tea exports hit a record high in 2025, doubling in one year. The US is the top destination market.
Supply
645
Tea farming households remaining in Kyoto Prefecture, down from 1,679 fifteen years ago. Supply is contracting.
Use-case mismatch
Most production is optimized for food manufacturing
Not straight drinking service. Most sourcing guides do not address this split.
Verification risk
No single standard governs grade or origin claims
Grade and provenance labels remain inconsistent across international wholesale channels.

How we help

Better sourcing decisions, before you place the order.

For cafés, hotels, restaurants, and beverage brands sourcing Japanese matcha in North America. We help operators verify origin, select the right grade for each use case, prepare the documentation stack, and build a supply plan that holds when tencha allocations tighten.

01

Origin Verification

What regional labels actually mean in Japan, and how to trace origin claims before the first payment. The Uji brand legally covers four prefectures. Most sourcing guides do not mention this.

02

Grade by Use Case

Latte, straight service, pastry, retail. Each requires different sourcing logic. Paying ceremonial prices for a latte program is a procurement error, not a quality decision.

03

Documentation

COA, organic JAS, FDA and EU pesticide MRL compliance. What each document covers, and what a capable supplier produces without being asked twice.

04

Supply Continuity

With Kyoto tencha supply contracting year on year, a backup supply plan is not optional for operators who depend on matcha.

Featured
Sourcing

Japan Filed for National GI Status on Japanese Tea. What It Means for Buyers.

Japan's tea industry filed a national GI application last October, a direct response to Chinese counterfeit Uji matcha, Chinese companies had filed over 3,000 trademark applications using "宇治" by 2019, blocking Japanese exports in those markets. If approved, this would be Japan's second national GI after sake. Green tea exports reached a record ¥72.1 billion in 2025. For North American buyers, what the GI covers and how verification will work for import orders is still being decided.

Tea Craft

"Ceremonial Grade" Has No Legal Definition in Japan. Here Is What Actually Matters.

No government body defines ceremonial grade. Producers set their own thresholds, and those lines differ considerably at the same price point. The checkable variables: shade duration, first harvest, tencha processing without kneading, stone milling under 60 rpm.

Tea Craft · May 2025 · 8 min
Sourcing

How to Source Matcha for Your Café Without Getting Burned

A latte-grade and a straight-service grade require entirely different sourcing logic. Most operators find out after the first batch arrives. Origin claims, grade matching, documentation, and the reseller risks most buyers encounter too late.

Sourcing · April 2025 · 11 min
Cafés

The Boutique Matcha Bar Model That Chain Operators Are Watching

A new generation of matcha-first spaces sources direct from named Japanese farms rather than corporate distributors. They show the documentation. The sourcing shapes what goes on the menu and what they can charge for it.

Cafés · March 2025 · 9 min
Supply

Kyoto Has Fewer Than 650 Tea Farmers Left. What Happens to Your Supply Chain Next.

Kyoto had 1,679 tea farming households fifteen years ago. By 2023 that number was 645, falling by roughly 40 per year. More than half are over 60 with no named successor. Tencha prices have fallen 30% from their 2017 peak as lower-cost national production expanded. Operators without a backup plan will find out what that means at their next ordering cycle.

Read more
Culture

Uji Is Not a Town. It Is a Brand That Legally Covers Four Prefectures.

The Kyoto Prefecture Tea Industry Council defines Uji matcha as tea finished by Kyoto-based businesses using leaves from Kyoto, Nara, Shiga, and Mie. Uji City itself has around 80 hectares of tea gardens. Buyers pay considerably more for Uji-labeled products. What the label covers and how to verify it for an import order are two different conversations.

Tea Craft

The Seven Production Variables That Actually Determine Matcha Quality

Producer, cultivar, origin, harvest timing, shade method, drying process, milling method. Each has real quality consequences and none reliably appear on packaging. Kyoto's Uji-variety cultivars trade at a 50% premium over standard yabukita, the amino acid profile is measurably different.

Tea Craft · February 2025 · 10 min
Procurement

The Documentation Your Matcha Supplier Should Produce in 48 Hours

Certificate of Analysis, organic JAS certification, pesticide residue testing against FDA and EU maximum residue levels. What each document covers and what a prepared supplier delivers without hesitation. Documentation gaps are the most common surprise when entering the US or EU market.

Procurement · January 2025 · 7 min
Sourcing

What Fake Reseller Risk Actually Looks Like Before You Place an Order

Origin claims that cannot be traced. COAs issued by the broker rather than the mill. Pricing that does not match the stated specification. No contact name at the production facility. These are not edge cases, they are the standard experience for buyers entering the market without contacts in Japan.

Sourcing · January 2025 · 6 min
On positioning

The premium tier of Japanese matcha has no credible owner in North America yet.

The market for matcha lattes is large and growing. The market for authenticated, properly sourced Japanese tea, served correctly, explained honestly, barely exists at scale. That gap is where Matcha Métier operates.
